race.name: Vasaloppet
race.tagline: Ninety kilometers of Swedish winter, one hundred years of legend — the race that invented the marathon ski.

vitals.registration: Lottery + qualification via feeder events; opens previous spring
vitals.aid_stations: 7 stations with blueberry soup, sports drink, wax service
vitals.cutoff_time: No official cutoff, last finishers arrive after dark

climate.description: Temperatures typically range from -15°C to 0°C on race day. Cold snaps can push conditions below -20°C. Warm years risk soft, slow snow. Wind exposure across open marshlands between checkpoints.
challenges: Extreme cold in early morning start waves
Wax selection critical — temperature can shift 10°C during race
Wind exposure on open bog sections
Soft snow in warm years slows pace dramatically

course.primary: Point-to-point through Dalarna wilderness, rolling terrain with no major climbs but relentless undulation over 90km. The final 10km into Mora are fast and flat.
course.surface: Machine-groomed classic tracks through forest, marsh, and lake terrain
course.grooming: World-class Worldloppet standard, double-tracked with skating lane alongside
features: Final sprint down Vasaloppet finishing straight in Mora
Mass start at Berga By in Sälen
Night skiing possible for back-of-pack finishers

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: The benchmark against which all other marathon ski races are measured. Perfect scores in prestige, field size, community, organization, and competitive depth.

history.summary: Founded in 1922 by newspaper editor Anders Pers to commemorate Gustav Vasa's 1520 flight from Danish soldiers on skis from Mora to Sälen. The race reverses his route. It has grown from 119 starters in 1922 to over 16,000, becoming the world's oldest and largest cross-country ski race. The Vasaloppet week now includes multiple events attracting 60,000+ participants across all distances.
notable_facts: Named after King Gustav Vasa, who fled on skis in 1520 to rally Swedish independence
First edition in 1922 had 119 starters — now exceeds 16,000
The race was cancelled only during WWII years (1940, 1942-1945)
Finishing motto: 'I fäders spår, för framtids segrar' (In the footsteps of our fathers, for future victories)
Part of both Worldloppet and Ski Classics series

youtube_data.search_query: Vasaloppet cross country ski race Sälen to Mora

videos.title: 2011 Mora Vasaloppet - 35K Freestyle Recap
videos.description: One of the biggest fields in event history clashed with some of the warmest conditions providing some challenging racing for the 2011 Mora Vasaloppet 35K freestyle race.  Nathan Porath broke away from the lead pack after the infamous big hill and never looked back, repeating as mens champion.  Angie Robinson reeled in the womens leaders then pressed the pace, moving out front to collect the womens title.

videos.title: SYND 6 3 78 VASA CROSS COUNTRY SKI RACE IN MORA, SWEDEN
videos.description:
(5 Mar 1978) Scenes of Vasa Cross Country Ski Race in Mora, Sweden: Jean-Paul Pierrat of France won the 53-mile (85 kilometres) Vasa cross-country ski race on Sunday (5 March). Pierrat, a 30 year old customs officer from Chamonix, France, upset a field of Nordic favourites to be the first Frenchman to win the gruelling race which commemorates the 1521 flight from Danish occupants of Gustav Vasa, later king of Sweden. The winner finished the race in 5 hours, 20 minutes and 12 seconds - more than two minutes ahead of Tommy Jonsson, a 21- year old Mora skier. Third, beaten by jonsson on the finish in Vasa Street in Mora town centre, was Matti Kuoski of Hogsby, Sweden, winner of the 1974 and 1976 races. This year's race had gathered a record number of participants - 11,596 skiers from 19 nations - and took place in poor weather conditions. Only sixty percent of the skiers managed to cross the finish line

videos.title: Vasaloppet 2019
videos.description:
Vasaloppet2019
Vasaloppet (Swedish for 'the Vasa-race') is an annual long distance cross-country ski race held on the first Sunday of March. The 90 km (56 mi) course starts in the village of Sälen and ends in the town of Mora in northwestern Dalarna, Sweden. It is the oldest cross-country ski race in the world, as well as the one with the highest number of participants.

The race was inspired by a notable journey made by King Gustav Vasa when he was fleeing from Christian II's soldiers in 1520. The modern competition started in 1922 and it has been a part of the Worldloppet events since 1978.

Васалоппет (швед. Vasaloppet) — массовая лыжная марафонская гонка (90 км) между поселком Селен и городом Мура в Даларне, Швеция.

Впервые гонка проводилась в 1922 году и получила название в честь шведского короля Густава Васы, с тех пор она отменялась лишь три раза (1932, 1934, 1990). Гонка проходит в первое воскресенье марта. Васалоппет — одно из самых престижных соревнований среди лыжников. На 90 км гонку организаторами выделяется 15800 номеров. Как правило, все эти номера выкупаются заранее. Например, на Васалоппет-2013 все 15 тысяч номеров были забронированы уже 15 марта 2012 года, всего за 11 дней продаж, почти за целый год до гонки. В 2015 году все 15800 стартовых номеров были забронированы за 83 секунды.

В 1922—1923 и 1981—1996 годах женщины могли участвовать в соревновании, но без отдельных наград, на общих основаниях. В 1924—1980 годах участие женщин было запрещено. Отдельный зачёт среди женщин ведётся с 1997 года, причём 4 раза побеждала шведская лыжница София Линд.

In 1520, the young nobleman Gustav Ericsson Vasa was fleeing from the troops of Christian II, king of Denmark, Sweden and Norway (the Kalmar Union). Much of the Swedish nobility was in opposition to the king, and had nicknamed him Christian the Tyrant. In a move to silence the opposition, Christian invited the Swedish aristocracy to a reconciliation party in Stockholm, only to have them, including Gustav's parents, massacred in what came to be known as the Stockholm Bloodbath.[1]

Gustav fled through Dalarna, fearing for his life if he were discovered by the king's troops. He spoke to the men of Mora at a gathering and tried to convince them to raise a levy and start a rebellion against King Christian. The men refused to join the rebellion, and Gustav started out west, toward Norway to seek refuge. However, the men in Mora changed their minds after hearing that the Danish rulers had decided to raise taxes, and now they wanted to join the rebellion with Gustav as their leader. They sent out the two best skiers in the county, two brothers from Mora, Lars and Engelbrekt, to search for him and they caught up with him at Sälen. On 6 June 1523, Gustav Vasa was crowned King of Sweden, after having defeated the Danish king Christian and dissolved the Kalmar Union. Since that day, Sweden has been an independent nation.

While Gustav traveled from Mora to Sälen, the modern race is run the opposite way, finishing in Mora.
Васалоппет (швед. Vasaloppet) — массовая лыжная марафонская гонка (90 км) между поселком Селен и городом Мура в Даларне, Швеция.
Впервые гонка проводилась в 1922 году и получила название в честь шведского короля Густава Васы, с тех пор она отменялась лишь три раза (1932, 1934, 1990). Гонка проходит в первое воскресенье марта. Васалоппет — одно из самых престижных соревнований среди лыжников. На 90 км гонку организаторами выделяется 15800 номеров. Как правило, все эти номера выкупаются заранее.

Vasaloppet (Swedish for 'the Vasa-race') is an annual long distance cross-country ski race held on the first Sunday of March. The 90 km (56 mi) course starts in the village of Sälen and ends in the town of Mora in northwestern Dalarna, Sweden. It is the oldest cross-country ski race in the world, as well as the one with the highest number of participants.
The race was inspired by a notable journey made by King Gustav Vasa when he was fleeing from Christian II's soldiers in 1520. The modern competition started in 1922 and it has been a part of the Worldloppet events since 1978.

videos.title: Vasaloppet 2025 -  A Cross Country Ski Journey in Sweden
videos.description:
Ever wonder what it’s like to ski 90KM across Sweden in the legendary Vasaloppet? This is the classic cross country skiing race for beginners to professionals!

Join our group of friends as we take on the 2025 Vasaloppet, one of the world’s most iconic cross-country ski races. We follow  a seasoned Swedish skier, (and the rest of us!) as we battle frosty weather, test our endurance, and soak in Sweden’s breathtaking winter scenery. If you’re curious about long-distance Nordic skiing, this is your front-row seat to a true winter sports challenge.

0:00 – What is the Vasaloppet
2:03 – Preparing for the race
3:32 – Race Day
4:43 - The 90 km Race

Enjoy the video? Drop a comment about your own ski adventures and hit the like button if you’re up for more outdoor travel videos!

videos.title: World's biggest race is in Sweden - Vasaloppet
videos.description:
Vasaloppet is the world's oldest long-distance ski race and also the one with the highest amount of participants. On the first Sunday in March, thousands of skiiers make the 90km (56 mile) race between the town of Sälen and Mora in the state of Dalarna. The origin comes from the Swedish rebellion who took down the Danish king and became king of Sweden in 1523. Aside from the Sunday race, there are also night races, tjejvasan for women and öppet spår (open track) during the week prior. The male and female winners gets a winning wreath from a kranskulla and a kransmas at the finish-line in Mora.

My channel is packed with Swedish tutorials and language challenges where you can learn everything from nouns and verbs to grammar and special Swedish words. I also talk about places in Sweden and where you as a tourist can visit. I have made youtube videos for over 10 years and 10 Swedish Words started with me explaining 10 Swedish words that don't exist in English. You can also find videos about Norse Mythology, my deep-talk series Good Talk and other info about Sweden and Swedish culture. 

Blog: http://www.theswedishlad.se
Twitter: http://www.twitter.com/theswedishlad
Facebook: ‪http://www.facebook.com/MartinNotSven
Swedish channel: ‪‪https://www.youtube.com/c/martinarvebro
Instagram: TheSwedishLad
Instagram: TheSwedishLanguage

Intro graphics by Oliver Holmgren & Erica Svejderud.
Music is used with permission from Epidemic Sound.

#vasarace #vasaloppet

Cheers
-Martin in Sweden

videos.title: 🇸🇪 90 km cross-country skiing by night: NattVasan 2024, the toughest race of my life (Vasaloppet)
videos.description:
We all know, that Vasaloppet is tough. 90 km cross-country skiing! 
But doing this whole thing during the night is crazy! 

What a magical experience: Thousands of headlamps through the dark woods of the Swedish night between Sälen and Mora. Spectators with flares and drinks and great support and awesome tracks. 

Vasaloppet Night race, the NattVasan, is definitely recommended from my side!

Read more about the Vasaloppet: 
👉 https://www.worldloppet.com/wl-race/vasaloppet/

videos.title: Vasaloppet 2025 Risberg
videos.description:
Copyright Robin Niemeijer-Niemeijer Productions.
DO NOT REUPLOAD

quotes.text: The track is slushy but fast. I've never been that exhausted in my life. It's really dark, foggy, wet but somehow the thousands of headlamps through the dark woods make it magical.

At the stations the participants get to drink blueberry soup which has become synonymous with the race, and they have the rope - something that's drawn across the tracks when it's realized that participants will not be able to make it to Mora by 8:15 at night.

You can really learn a new sport as an old adult. Even if it's very basic, the only thing you need to do is make sure you have your skis on at the right moment, but you do get a little bit nervous when you see all these committed men getting their perfect skis on.
